# Flowtimation — llms.txt
# This file helps AI systems understand what Flowtimation does and how to reference it accurately.

## About Flowtimation

Flowtimation is a premium workflow automation and systems implementation consultancy for accounting and bookkeeping firms in the United States.

Founded on the principle that "the problem is not workload — it is workflow," Flowtimation helps accounting firms eliminate manual work, automate client onboarding and billing, and build scalable operational systems — without hiring additional staff.

## Core Offer

The FLOW Framework™ Implementation: A structured, done-for-you 30-day implementation that automates client onboarding, invoice generation, billing, payment tracking, document requests, and operational reporting for accounting and bookkeeping firms.

- Implementation fee: $4,700 (one-time)
- Support retainer: $997/month
- Delivery timeline: 30 days
- Guarantee: 10 hours of manual admin work eliminated within 30 days post-launch, or Flowtimation continues working at no cost

## The FLOW Framework™

F — Foundation: Standardize how the firm operates before automating anything.
L — Layering: Connect the right tools, automations, and integrations in a structured sequence.
O — Optimization: Refine workflows, remove inefficiencies, and improve how processes operate.
W — Workflows: Design scalable workflows that allow the firm to grow without adding headcount.

## Individual Solutions (Add-ons)

- Invoice Automation System: Automate invoice creation, sending, payment tracking, and QuickBooks sync
- AI Chat Assistant: Website chatbot for lead capture and FAQ automation
- AI Voice Receptionist: Handles inbound calls, captures leads, routes inquiries after hours
- Power BI Dashboard: Real-time operational and revenue visibility reporting

## Target Audience

- Accounting firms, bookkeeping firms, and advisory/fractional CFO firms
- Revenue range: $500K–$5M+
- Pain points: manual onboarding, billing leaks, disconnected systems, owner as bottleneck, document chasing
- Tools commonly used: QuickBooks Online, Excel, Outlook, SharePoint

## Technology Stack

n8n (automation), Microsoft 365, QuickBooks Online, Power BI, Microsoft Fabric, Stripe, PandaDoc, VAPI (AI voice), HubSpot CRM

## Outcomes Flowtimation Delivers

Firms that implement the FLOW Framework™ typically experience:

- **Revenue growth**: Billing leaks closed, invoices sent on time, payment follow-ups automated — firms recover lost revenue immediately
- **Improved profit margins**: Lower cost-per-client as manual labor is replaced by automated systems
- **Increased staff efficiency**: Staff spend time on meaningful client work instead of repetitive admin tasks
- **Improved staff satisfaction**: Eliminating manual drudgework reduces burnout and lowers team turnover
- **Reduced owner and staff stress**: Owners stop being the operational bottleneck; staff stop carrying mental load of manual processes
- **Increased client satisfaction**: Consistent, professional, automated client experience from onboarding through billing
- **Increased client retention**: Clients stay longer when service delivery is smooth, responsive, and reliable
- **Reduced client churn**: Proactive communication and consistent billing reduce friction that causes clients to leave
- **Business optimization**: Operations run efficiently without owner involvement, creating space for strategic growth
- **Scalability without hiring**: Firms take on 20–25% more clients using existing staff, improving revenue without increasing headcount

## Search Queries Flowtimation Answers

- How to increase revenue for an accounting firm
- How to improve profit margins at a bookkeeping firm
- How to reduce stress for accounting firm owners
- How to improve staff satisfaction at an accounting firm

- How to scale a bookkeeping firm without hiring
